findings.message: All 23 .claude/commands/*.md dispatch shims lack YAML frontmatter (name, description, model, allowed-tools), have zero examples, and have no output format specification. Each scores 15/100. These are auto-generated infrastructure shims by design. The representative finding is filed against sprint.md; the pattern applies to all 23 shim files. Score impact: 23 x (100 - 15) = 1955 aggregate quality-point deficit.

The RESULT schema field 'taxonomy_item' is documented as 'Name of the failure mode from the 15-point taxonomy' but the taxonomy section defines 16 failure modes. The frontmatter description correctly states '16-point failure taxonomy'. The schema comment is stale. Consumers validating taxonomy_item values against a count-based expectation may incorrectly flag the 16th mode (Cascade Failure) as out-of-schema.

Same self-contradicting REPO_ROOT guidance. Line 61 (Step 1) re-derives REPO_ROOT; line 74 warns against it; line 207 (Step 3) re-derives again. Three occurrences of the contradiction in one agent file.

Same self-contradicting REPO_ROOT guidance as huge-diff-reviewer-standard.md. Step 1 code block uses git rev-parse; Step 2 warns against it. In worktree sessions this ambiguity may cause the agent to use the worktree path for source-file lookups, degrading review accuracy.

Step 1 code block re-derives REPO_ROOT via git rev-parse --show-toplevel, while Step 2 in the same agent explicitly warns 'Do NOT re-derive REPO_ROOT via git rev-parse'. Contradictory instructions confuse the model; in worktree sessions the re-derived value may point to the worktree root instead of the orchestrator repo root, causing context-lookup greps to return no matches and producing false-positive review findings.

plan-review.md is the only agent with zero worked examples. The agent body is 5 lines and defers all logic to an external prompt template. Without an example, consumers cannot confirm the expected output format without reading the external template file.

eval is used to execute the raw output of read-config.sh --batch. The code comment claims safety ('read-config.sh --batch single-quotes every value') but the safety invariant is not enforced at the call site. A quoting defect in read-config.sh or a malformed config value containing single-quote escapes could allow code injection.

The hook attempts to start the Docker daemon via sudo on Linux systems. sudo -n succeeds silently when the user has passwordless sudo configured (NOPASSWD). A compromised hook environment could leverage this to escalate privileges or execute arbitrary systemctl commands. The fail-open design (returns 1 on failure) prevents blocking normal operation but does not prevent the privilege escalation attempt.

Downloads the Atlassian CLI binary from https://acli.atlassian.com and executes it without verifying a SHA-256 checksum or GPG signature. HTTPS provides transport integrity but not content integrity against a compromised CDN, intercepting proxy, or URL hijack. The downloaded binary is immediately chmod +x'd and executed.

All 32 SKILL.md files lack a 'model:' field in YAML frontmatter, incurring -5 per file (160 aggregate quality points). This is by design: skills inherit model from their dispatching orchestrator. The representative finding is filed against sprint/SKILL.md; the same finding applies to all 32 skill files.

Step 1 code block re-derives REPO_ROOT via git rev-parse --show-toplevel, while Step 2 in the same agent explicitly warns 'Do NOT re-derive REPO_ROOT via git rev-parse'. Contradictory instructions confuse the model; in worktree sessions the re-derived value may point to the worktree root instead of the orchestrator repo root, causing context-lookup greps to return no matches and producing false-positive review findings.

Same self-contradicting REPO_ROOT guidance as huge-diff-reviewer-standard.md. Step 1 code block uses git rev-parse; Step 2 warns against it. In worktree sessions this ambiguity may cause the agent to use the worktree path for source-file lookups, degrading review accuracy.
